'''Darren Abram''' (born 27 September 1967) is a [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1980s and 1990s, and [[Coach (sport)|coach]]ed in the 2000s. He played at club level for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]] (1987–88), [[Blackpool Borough#Springfield Borough|Springfield Borough]] (1988–90), the [[Rochdale Hornets]] (1990–91), [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]] (1993–97), [[Chorley Lynx#Lancashire Lynx (1997–2000)|Lancashire Lynx]] (1998), as a {{rlp|ce}}, and coached at club level for [[Chorley Lynx]], the [[Leigh Centurions]] (2003–05), and the [[Rochdale Hornets]] (2006–07).<ref name="Coach Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>

==Playing career==
Abram started his career at [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]], making one appearance in 1987.<ref>{{cite web|title=Abram, Darren|url=http://wolvesplayers.thisiswarrington.co.uk/results.asp?code=2|work=wolvesplayers.thisiswarrington.co.uk|publisher=Warrington Guardian|accessdate=20 February 2014|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20140225023719/http://wolvesplayers.thisiswarrington.co.uk/results.asp?code=2|archivedate=25 February 2014}}</ref> In 1988, he spent a month on loan at [[Swinton Lions|Swinton]],<ref>{{cite news |title=Sport In Brief |work=The Guardian |date=7 January 1988 |location=London |page=27 |id={{ProQuest|186767583}}}}</ref> before being transferred to [[Blackpool Borough#Springfield Borough|Chorley Borough]] later that year.

Darren Abram played, and scored a [[Try (rugby)|try]] in [[Rochdale Hornets]] 14–24 defeat by [[St Helens R.F.C.|St. Helens]] in the [[1991 Lancashire Cup]] Final during the [[1991–92 Rugby Football League season|1991–92 season]] at [[Wilderspool Stadium]], [[Warrington]], on Sunday 20 October 1991.

==Coaching career==
Abram cut his coaching teeth with [[Chorley Lynx]] in National League Two and was taken on by the Leigh Centurions, with a view to taking them into the [[Super League]], in October 2003.<ref>{{cite web |title=Centurions appoint new head coach |url=https://www.lancashiretelegraph.co.uk/news/5877557.centurions-appoint-new-head-coach/ |website=Lancashire Telegraph |access-date=3 January 2024 |date=13 October 2003}}</ref> He accomplished this in his first season as Leigh Centurions beat Whitehaven in a dramatic [[RFL Championship|National League One]] Grand Final. Leigh Centurions' Super League season of 2005, however, proved a disaster and the club were relegated after winning just two games, with Abram leaving in August 2005.<ref name="BBC">"[https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/leigh/4737993.stm Coach Abram leaves post at Leigh]", [[BBC]], 2 August 2005, retrieved 3 October 2010</ref> He was appointed Rochdale Hornets' coach in November 2005, as successor to [[Bobbie Goulding]], who had resigned two weeks earlier.<ref name="Partington">Partington, Richard (2005) "[http://menmedia.co.uk/manchestereveningnews/sport/rugby_league/rochdale_hornets/s/506620_abram_takes_the_reins_at_hornets Abram takes the reins at hornets] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20121112160523/http://menmedia.co.uk/manchestereveningnews/sport/rugby_league/rochdale_hornets/s/506620_abram_takes_the_reins_at_hornets |date=12 November 2012 }}", ''[[Manchester Evening News]]'', 29 November 2005, retrieved 3 October 2010</ref> Abram parted company from Rochdale Hornets in July 2007.

==Personal life==
Darren Abram is the father of the rugby league {{rlp|fb|wg|so|sh}} who has played in the 2010s and 2020s for [[Barrow Raiders]], [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Oldham R.L.F.C.|Oldham]]; Dan Abram.<ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/>
